description: A screenshot styled as a Wall Street Journal online article, presented as a designed share-card. At top a small blue 'ECONOMY' section kicker sits above the bold black serif headline 'Central U.S. Manufacturing Activity Accelerates'. A deck/subhead in gray reads 'Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City says its Tenth District Manufacturing Survey's composite index climbed to 8 this month from 6 in October'. Below is a byline 'By Ed Frankl' with a blue 'Follow' button, timestamp 'Nov. 20, 2025 12:03 pm ET', and a row of WSJ UI controls (Share, Resize, Listen (1 min), and a three-dot menu). The lower half is a color photograph inside an industrial setting: a man in a maroon T-shirt and gray Nike cap, wearing a work glove, working on a large disassembled engine/transmission assembly suspended by a chain hoist; shelving with parts boxes (a red toolbox, a 'Z-03' bin label) recedes behind him on a factory floor. The image is essentially a credibility-borrowing repost of a mainstream-media business story to support the tweet's 'THE TRUMP EFFECT' manufacturing-growth narrative. Target audience: general public, signaling economic momentum under the administration. No government seal is on the image.
notable_text: ECONOMY; Central U.S. Manufacturing Activity Accelerates; Tenth District Manufacturing Survey's composite index climbed to 8 this month from 6 in October; By Ed Frankl, Nov. 20, 2025
